items.title: NZD/USD recovers slightly from daily low, keeps the red above 0.5800 on firmer USD
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair opens with a bearish gap at the start of a new trading week in reaction to failed US-Iran peace talks over the weekend and trades around the 0.5800 mark during the Asian session.

items.title: Canadian Dollar falls further as USD haven demand counters WTI surge
items.summary: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) is extending its pullback from two-week highs of 1.3844 against the US Dollar (USD) early Monday, with USD/CAD capitalizing on the renewed demand for the Greenback as a go-to safe-haven.

items.title: WTI jumps roughly 8% toward $100 as US blockades Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) – the US oil benchmark – has opened the week with a bullish gap, climbing roughly 8%, looking to retarget the $100 threshold.

items.title: Japanese Yen falls as US Dollar rises after US–Iran talks collapse
items.summary: USD/JPY extends its winning streak for the third successive day, trading around 159.80 during the Asian hours on Monday.

items.title: US President Trump considers limited strikes on Iran after talks collapse - WSJ
items.summary: Citing officials and people familiar with the situation, the Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported on Monday that White House advisers are considering resuming limited military strikes in Iran along with the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as a way to break a deadlock in peace talks.

items.title: Iran’s Foreign Minister Araghchi: Engaged with the US in good faith to end the war
items.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi said early Monday that “Iran engaged with the US in good faith to end the war.”

items.title: China: Inflation pressures build with energy – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chief Economist for Greater China, Lynn Song, notes that China’s CPI inflation eased to 1.0% year-on-year after Lunar New Year, while PPI turned positive for the first time since 2022.

items.title: Malaysia: Solid GDP and contained inflation – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research expects Malaysia’s 1Q26 advance Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to grow 5.5% year-on-year, slightly below 6.3% in 4Q25 but still robust. Growth is seen supported by export-oriented electrical and electronics manufacturing, global AI tailwinds, construction and domestic demand.

items.title: CNY: Trade normalization and growth risks – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities expects China’s March exports to normalize after a strong Jan–Feb report, while imports could surprise on the upside as authorities stockpile key goods and commodities during the US–Iran conflict. Rising input costs may slow production and weigh on exports.

items.title: KRW: War risk keeps 1,450–1,550 range in focus – ING
items.summary: ING’s Min Joo Kang notes that KRW is trading below 1,500, with near-term moves heavily dependent on Middle East developments. The team keeps its 1,450–1,550 trading range, expecting KRW to strengthen rapidly if the war ends.

items.title: Singapore: Exports boosted by electronics cycle – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research expects Singapore’s non-oil domestic exports to rise for a seventh consecutive month in March 2026, accelerating to 10.3% year-on-year from 4.0% in February.

items.title: Fed’s Daly: If inflation stays elevated, we would hold steady
items.summary: Mary Daly, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of San Francisco, told Reuters in an interview on Friday that if Iran conflict is resolved quickly and Oil prices come back down, a rate cut may not be out of the question.
